phrases.intent_en: Ask how to get to the hilltop temple
phrases.text_local: Comment monte-t-on jusqu'au temple du belvédère ?
phrases.pronunciation: koh-MAHN mohnt-TOHN zhoos-KOH tahm-PLUH doo bel-vay-DEHR
phrases.cultural_note: The Temple de la Sibylle sits on the Île du Belvédère, an island in the lake accessed by two bridges. From the top, you have a panoramic view of Paris including Sacré-Cœur to the southwest. The climb is steep but short — about 5 minutes from the lake level.

responses.english: Go up the main path, the temple is at the top.
responses.phonetic: mohn-TAY par luh shuh-MAN pran-see-PAL, luh tahm-pluh eh tahn oh
responses.local: Montez par le chemin principal, le temple est en haut.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if dogs are allowed in the park
phrases.text_local: Les chiens sont-ils autorisés dans le parc ?
phrases.pronunciation: lay SHYEHN sohn-TEEL oh-toh-ree-ZAY dahn luh PARK
phrases.cultural_note: Dogs are welcome in Parc des Buttes-Chaumont and must be kept on a leash. The park is very popular with local dog owners. There are several water fountains for dogs. The park is a beloved neighborhood green space in the 19th arrondissement, used mostly by locals rather than tourists.

responses.english: Yes, dogs are allowed on a leash.
responses.phonetic: wee, lay shyehn sohn toh-toh-ree-ZAY ahn less
responses.local: Oui, les chiens sont autorisés en laisse.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for the best spot for a picnic with a view
phrases.text_local: Quel est le meilleur endroit pour un pique-nique avec vue sur Paris ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel eh luh meh-YUR ahn-DRWAH poor uhn peek-NEEK ah-VEK voo soor pah-REE
phrases.cultural_note: The grassy slopes below the temple and the lawns along the east side of the lake offer excellent views. The park was created by Haussmann in 1867 from what was previously a gypsum quarry and rubbish dump — its transformation is considered one of the great landscape achievements of Second Empire Paris.

responses.english: The lawn near the lake has a nice view.
responses.phonetic: lah puh-LOOZ preh doo lak ah oon bel voo
responses.local: La pelouse près du lac a une belle vue.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where Rosa Bonheur restaurant and bar is
phrases.text_local: Où se trouve le guinguette Rosa Bonheur ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo suh TROOV luh gehn-GET roh-ZAH boh-NUR
phrases.cultural_note: Rosa Bonheur is an outdoor bar and restaurant within the park, open Wednesday to Sunday. It's a 'guinguette' — a traditional open-air French dance café — and extremely popular with young Parisians on summer evenings. Arrive early on weekends as it fills quickly.

responses.english: Rosa Bonheur is in the middle of the park, on the hill.
responses.phonetic: roh-ZAH boh-NUHR eh toh mee-LYUH doo park, soor lah koh-LEEN
responses.local: Rosa Bonheur est au milieu du parc, sur la colline.

phrases.intent_en: Ask which entrance is closest to the Buttes-Chaumont metro station
phrases.text_local: Quelle entrée est la plus proche du métro Buttes-Chaumont ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel ahn-TRAY eh lah ploo PROSH doo may-TROH boot-shoh-MOHN
phrases.cultural_note: The park has several entrances. The main entrance near Métro Buttes-Chaumont (Line 7bis) is on the west side of the park. The north entrance on Rue de Crimée leads to the dramatic suspension bridges over the lake. Line 7bis also serves Botzaris station at the south entrance.

responses.english: The nearest entrance is on Rue Botzaris.
responses.phonetic: lan-TRAY lah ploo prosh eh soor lah roo bot-zah-REES
responses.local: L'entrée la plus proche est sur la Rue Botzaris.
